Ruminating thoughts, repetitive mistakes, burnout from grinding, bad habits
Compulsion
Sunk Cost Fallacy
Bounded loop (fixed count)
Conditional loop (checks state)
while(true) - no exit
Act first then check
"I allow myself [X] minutes, then STOP"
"I do this max [N] times, then move on"
"When [trigger], I do [alternative] instead"
Remove the trigger entirely (delete app, change location)
Is this loop still serving me?
Is the output of this loop improving, flat, or declining?
Is the effort worth the output? (Profit Factor)
Should I continue, modify, or break this loop?
Am I running this loop by choice or by compulsion?
After [EXISTING LOOP], I add [NEW ACTION].
Work in 25-min loops with 5-min breaks. Every 4 loops (2h), take 15-30 min to evaluate. Built-in checkpoint system.

I've been doing this for [time] → That time is gone regardless. The question is: Does the NEXT iteration of this loop add value? If no → break. The past iterations don't make the next one worth doing.
